---
title: "localtime extension example"
format: html
---

The `localtime` shortcode displays a given date and time in the reader's local timezone.
Times on this page update to your local time when the page loads.

## In context

Here is how the shortcode looks inline in prose:

> Join us for our next meetup, which starts at {{< localtime 2026-09-18 14:00 UTC format="full" >}}.

## Basic usage

| Shortcode | Renders locally as |
|---|---|
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 EST */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 EST >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 -05:00 */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 -05:00 >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-06-15 09:00 UTC */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-06-15 09:00 UTC >}} |

## Timezone input formats

Three formats refer to the same timezone — abbreviation, IANA name, and UTC offset.
These three shortcodes all produce the same output:

| Shortcode | Renders locally as |
|---|---|
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 EST */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 EST >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 America/New_York */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 America/New_York >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 -05:00 */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 -05:00 >}} |

IANA names are unambiguous and recommended when precision matters. UTC offsets are useful for timezones without a standard abbreviation or for regions that don't observe DST.

## DST handling

DST is handled automatically in the reader's browser. Abbreviations like `EST` and `CET` are mapped to their IANA timezone, so the browser applies the correct offset for each date — winter dates use the standard offset, summer dates use the DST offset.

| Shortcode | Renders locally as |
|---|---|
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-15 14:00 CET */>}}` (winter) | {{< localtime 2026-01-15 14:00 CET >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-07-15 14:00 CET */>}}` (summer) | {{< localtime 2026-07-15 14:00 CET >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-15 09:00 EST */>}}` (winter) | {{< localtime 2026-01-15 09:00 EST >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-07-15 09:00 EST */>}}` (summer) | {{< localtime 2026-07-15 09:00 EST >}} |

The winter and summer rows for the same abbreviation convert to different UTC offsets (e.g. CET vs CEST, EST vs EDT).

## 12-hour time input

The time argument can be given in 12-hour format by appending `am` or `pm`. These two syntaxes — attached and space-separated — produce identical output:

| Shortcode | Renders locally as |
|---|---|
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 1:00pm EST */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 1:00pm EST >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 1:00 PM EST */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 1:00 PM EST >}} |

Both are accepted syntaxes; `am`/`pm` is case-insensitive.

## Format presets

Use the `format` argument to control the output. If omitted, `datetime` is used.

| Shortcode | Renders locally as |
|---|---|
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="datetime" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="datetime" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="date" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="date" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="time" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="time" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="time12" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="time12" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="datetime12" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="datetime12" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="full" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="full" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="full12" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="full12" >}} |

The `12` variants use 12-hour clock with no leading zero and lowercase am/pm (e.g. `1:00pm`).

## Custom format strings

Use `%`-style tokens to build your own format. The `-` flag removes zero-padding.

| Shortcode | Renders locally as |
|---|---|
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="%d %B at %H:%M" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="%d %B at %H:%M" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="%A, %d %B %Y" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="%A, %d %B %Y" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="%H:%M %Z" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-30 13:00 UTC format="%H:%M %Z" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-03 08:05 UTC format="%d %B %Y at %H:%M" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-03 08:05 UTC format="%d %B %Y at %H:%M" >}} |
| `{{</* localtime 2026-01-03 08:05 UTC format="%-d %B %Y at %-H:%M" */>}}` | {{< localtime 2026-01-03 08:05 UTC format="%-d %B %Y at %-H:%M" >}} |

The last two rows use the same date (`2026-01-03`) and time (`08:05`) — the first with zero-padded `%d` and `%H`, the second with no-padding `%-d` and `%-H`.
